questions.stem:
—______ you ______ your homework yet?
—Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 【解析】第一空根据副词yet（已经，还）可知要用现在完成时，结构为Have/Has+过去分词，主语是you，助动词用Have；第二空根据ago（……之前）是一般过去时的标志词，谓语动词用过去式finished。A项第一空一般过去时不能与yet连用；C项第二空have finished与ago矛盾；D项第一空Did与yet不搭配且第二空have finished与ago矛盾。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is standing under the tree is my classmate.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查定语从句关系词。先行词The boy指人，排除指物的which(B)和不能引导定语从句的what(C)。关系词在从句中作主语（is standing），who和whom的区别在于：who作主语，whom作宾语。此处需要主格，故选A。口诀：先行词指人作主语，关系代词用who；作宾语可用whom/who/that。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These books ______ to the school library when I finish reading them.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查一般将来时的被动语态。主语These books与谓语动词return之间是被动关系（书被归还），必须用被动语态，排除A和C。when引导的时间状语从句用一般现在时（finish）表示将来，主句应用一般将来时，结构为will be+过去分词，故选B。D项是一般过去时的被动语态，时态不符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It ______ be him. He has gone to Shanghai.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查情态动词表推测。根据后句He has gone to Shanghai（他去上海了，现在还没回来）可知，敲门的"不可能"是他。can't表示"不可能"，用于否定推测；must表示"一定"（肯定推测）；might/could表示"可能"（不确定）。根据语境，选C。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The weather was terrible yesterday. It was raining heavily and the wind was blowing ______.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查副词词义辨析。形容风"大/强"要用副词strongly，这是固定搭配。A. slowly（缓慢地）形容速度慢；B. quietly（安静地）形容声音；D. quickly（快速地）形容动作迅速。形容风、雨大常用strong/heavy，故选C。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We need to ______ a solution to the pollution problem before it's too late.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查动词短语辨析。come up with意为"想出，提出（主意、计划、解决方案等）"，与solution（解决方案）搭配恰当。A. look for（寻找）强调过程；C. get on with（与……相处/继续做）；D. take care of（照顾）。根据语境，我们需要"想出"解决办法，选B。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: With the ______ of technology, our life has changed a lot in the past ten years.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查词性转换。定冠词the后必须接名词，构成the...of结构。develop是动词，developing和developed是现在分词/过去分词/形容词，development是名词（发展）。句意：随着科技的发展……，故选D。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查介词搭配。read books about...表示"阅读关于……的书籍"，about意为"关于"。with（和……一起/用），for（为了），to（向，到）均不符合语境。句意：我们在网上搜索信息并阅读了很多关于机器人的书籍。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held a science fair. All the students were asked to work in groups and create something useful. My group decided to make a robot that could help clean the classroom.

At first, we had no idea how to start. We searched for information on the Internet and read many books (8)______ robots. Our teacher, Mr. Li, gave us some useful advice. He told us that we should use (9)______ materials to protect the environment.

We spent two weeks building the robot. It was not easy. Sometimes the robot didn't move (10)______ we expected. We had to test it again and again. During this process, we learned that teamwork was very important.

On the day of the exhibition, many students gathered around our artwork and took photos. We felt very (11)______ when the judges gave us the first prize.

This experience taught me (12)______ science is not just about knowledge in books. It is about solving problems and creating new things.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查语境推理。根据后文"to protect the environment（为了保护环境）"可知，应该使用"回收的"材料。recycled（回收的）符合环保主题。expensive（昂贵的）、natural（自然的）、colorful（多彩的）与环保关联不大。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held a science fair. All the students were asked to work in groups and create something useful. My group decided to make a robot that could help clean the classroom.

At first, we had no idea how to start. We searched for information on the Internet and read many books (8)______ robots. Our teacher, Mr. Li, gave us some useful advice. He told us that we should use (9)______ materials to protect the environment.

We spent two weeks building the robot. It was not easy. Sometimes the robot didn't move (10)______ we expected. We had to test it again and again. During this process, we learned that teamwork was very important.

On the day of the exhibition, many students gathered around our artwork and took photos. We felt very (11)______ when the judges gave us the first prize.

This experience taught me (12)______ science is not just about knowledge in books. It is about solving problems and creating new things.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查连词用法。as作为连词，引导方式状语从句，意为"按照，如同"，后接句子（we expected）。like作为介词，后接名词/代词，不接句子。when（当……时）和if（如果）逻辑不符。句意：有时机器人不像我们预期的那样移动。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held a science fair. All the students were asked to work in groups and create something useful. My group decided to make a robot that could help clean the classroom.

At first, we had no idea how to start. We searched for information on the Internet and read many books (8)______ robots. Our teacher, Mr. Li, gave us some useful advice. He told us that we should use (9)______ materials to protect the environment.

We spent two weeks building the robot. It was not easy. Sometimes the robot didn't move (10)______ we expected. We had to test it again and again. During this process, we learned that teamwork was very important.

On the day of the exhibition, many students gathered around our artwork and took photos. We felt very (11)______ when the judges gave us the first prize.

This experience taught me (12)______ science is not just about knowledge in books. It is about solving problems and creating new things.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查情感线索。根据后文"the judges gave us the first prize（评委给了我们一等奖）"可知，获奖后应该感到"自豪"。proud（自豪的）符合语境。nervous（紧张的）、tired（累的）、worried（担心的）均为负面情绪，与获奖情境不符。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held a science fair. All the students were asked to work in groups and create something useful. My group decided to make a robot that could help clean the classroom.

At first, we had no idea how to start. We searched for information on the Internet and read many books (8)______ robots. Our teacher, Mr. Li, gave us some useful advice. He told us that we should use (9)______ materials to protect the environment.

We spent two weeks building the robot. It was not easy. Sometimes the robot didn't move (10)______ we expected. We had to test it again and again. During this process, we learned that teamwork was very important.

On the day of the exhibition, many students gathered around our artwork and took photos. We felt very (11)______ when the judges gave us the first prize.

This experience taught me (12)______ science is not just about knowledge in books. It is about solving problems and creating new things.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查宾语从句连接词。taught me后为宾语从句，从句"science is not just about knowledge in books"结构完整，不缺成分，用that引导（that可省略，但选项中有that应优先选）。what在从句中作主语/宾语；which（哪一个）有选择含义；how（如何）作状语，均不符合。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school held a science fair. All the students were asked to work in groups and create something useful. My group decided to make a robot that could help clean the classroom.

At first, we had no idea how to start. We searched for information on the Internet and read many books (8)______ robots. Our teacher, Mr. Li, gave us some useful advice. He told us that we should use (9)______ materials to protect the environment.

We spent two weeks building the robot. It was not easy. Sometimes the robot didn't move (10)______ we expected. We had to test it again and again. During this process, we learned that teamwork was very important.

On the day of the exhibition, many students gathered around our artwork and took photos. We felt very (11)______ when the judges gave us the first prize.

This experience taught me (12)______ science is not just about knowledge in books. It is about solving problems and creating new things.

questions.stem: Where was the enzyme first discovered according to the passage?
options: A. In a laboratory in America.
B. In a landfill site in Japan.
C. In the ocean near Australia.
D. In a recycling factory in China.
questions.explanation: 【解析】细节理解题。根据第二段第一句"The enzyme was first found in a landfill site in Japan."可知，这种酶最初是在日本的一个垃圾填埋场发现的。A、C、D项地点均与原文不符。做细节题要准确定位关键词enzyme first found。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Scientists have found a way to help solve the plastic waste problem. They discovered an enzyme (酶) that can break down plastic bottles in just a few days. This is much faster than the hundreds of years it normally takes for plastic to decompose (分解) naturally.

The enzyme was first found in a landfill site in Japan. Scientists modified (改良) it to make it work better. In experiments, it reduced a plastic bottle to simple chemical building blocks in 48 hours. These blocks can be used to make new plastic products.

"This is a big step forward," said Professor John McGeehan, who led the research. "If we can make this enzyme work on a large scale, it could change the way we recycle plastic."

However, some experts warn that this technology is still in its early stages. It may take years before it can be used in real life. Also, we should not rely only on technology to solve the problem. Reducing plastic use is still the most important thing.

The discovery shows how science can help protect our environment. But the best way to deal with pollution is to stop creating so much waste in the first place.

questions.stem: What does the underlined word "decompose" in Paragraph 1 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 【解析】词义猜测题。根据上下文，酶能在几天内break down（分解）塑料瓶，这比自然decompose需要数百年要快得多。break down与decompose为同义关系，都指塑料被分解。且后文提到酶将瓶子reduced to simple chemical building blocks（还原为简单化学构件），进一步验证是"分解"之意。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Scientists have found a way to help solve the plastic waste problem. They discovered an enzyme (酶) that can break down plastic bottles in just a few days. This is much faster than the hundreds of years it normally takes for plastic to decompose (分解) naturally.

The enzyme was first found in a landfill site in Japan. Scientists modified (改良) it to make it work better. In experiments, it reduced a plastic bottle to simple chemical building blocks in 48 hours. These blocks can be used to make new plastic products.

"This is a big step forward," said Professor John McGeehan, who led the research. "If we can make this enzyme work on a large scale, it could change the way we recycle plastic."

However, some experts warn that this technology is still in its early stages. It may take years before it can be used in real life. Also, we should not rely only on technology to solve the problem. Reducing plastic use is still the most important thing.

The discovery shows how science can help protect our environment. But the best way to deal with pollution is to stop creating so much waste in the first place.

questions.stem: What can we infer from Professor John McGeehan's words in Paragraph 3?
options: A. The enzyme is already widely used around the world.
B. The discovery may bring great changes to plastic recycling.
C. The research on enzymes has completely failed.
D. Plastic pollution is no longer a serious problem now.
questions.explanation: 【解析】推理判断题。根据教授的话"This is a big step forward（这是向前迈出的一大步）"和"it could change the way we recycle plastic（它可能改变我们回收塑料的方式）"可推知，这项发现具有巨大潜力，可能给塑料回收带来变革。A项"已广泛应用"与后文still in its early stages矛盾；C项"完全失败"与big step forward相反；D项"不再是严重问题"文中未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Scientists have found a way to help solve the plastic waste problem. They discovered an enzyme (酶) that can break down plastic bottles in just a few days. This is much faster than the hundreds of years it normally takes for plastic to decompose (分解) naturally.

The enzyme was first found in a landfill site in Japan. Scientists modified (改良) it to make it work better. In experiments, it reduced a plastic bottle to simple chemical building blocks in 48 hours. These blocks can be used to make new plastic products.

"This is a big step forward," said Professor John McGeehan, who led the research. "If we can make this enzyme work on a large scale, it could change the way we recycle plastic."

However, some experts warn that this technology is still in its early stages. It may take years before it can be used in real life. Also, we should not rely only on technology to solve the problem. Reducing plastic use is still the most important thing.

The discovery shows how science can help protect our environment. But the best way to deal with pollution is to stop creating so much waste in the first place.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. How to Make Plastic Bottles
B. Scientists Find Enzyme to Break Down Plastic
C. The History of Plastic Waste
D. Ways to Reduce Plastic Use in Daily Life
questions.explanation: 【解析】主旨大意题。文章首段即点明主旨：科学家发现了一种能快速分解塑料的酶，后文围绕这一发现展开。A项"如何制作塑料瓶"偏离主题；C项"塑料垃圾的历史"文中未涉及；D项"减少塑料使用的方法"只是文末提及的次要信息。B项准确概括了文章核心内容——科学家发现分解塑料的酶。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Scientists have found a way to help solve the plastic waste problem. They discovered an enzyme (酶) that can break down plastic bottles in just a few days. This is much faster than the hundreds of years it normally takes for plastic to decompose (分解) naturally.

The enzyme was first found in a landfill site in Japan. Scientists modified (改良) it to make it work better. In experiments, it reduced a plastic bottle to simple chemical building blocks in 48 hours. These blocks can be used to make new plastic products.

"This is a big step forward," said Professor John McGeehan, who led the research. "If we can make this enzyme work on a large scale, it could change the way we recycle plastic."

However, some experts warn that this technology is still in its early stages. It may take years before it can be used in real life. Also, we should not rely only on technology to solve the problem. Reducing plastic use is still the most important thing.

The discovery shows how science can help protect our environment. But the best way to deal with pollution is to stop creating so much waste in the first place.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查逻辑关系。前文说"我过去是个夜猫子，总是起得很晚"，后文说"我经常上学迟到，整天感到累"，这是因果关系，用As a result（结果是）连接。However表转折，For example举例，Then表时间顺序，Now表现在，均不符合此处因果逻辑。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
I used to be a night owl and always woke up late. (17)______, I was often late for school and felt tired all day. Last month, I decided to change my lifestyle.

(18)______, I started going to bed at 10 p.m. and waking up at 6 a.m. At first, it was difficult to fall asleep so early. I listened to soft music and stopped using my phone before bed.

(19)______, instead of rushing to school without breakfast, I have time to enjoy a healthy meal. I also do some exercise in the morning, which makes me feel energetic.

The result is amazing. (20)______, I can focus better in class and have more time for hobbies after school. Changing my morning routine has really improved my life.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查时间顺序。前文说"上个月我决定改变生活方式"，后文说"我开始晚上10点睡觉，早上6点起床"，这是改变后的具体行动，用Then（然后）表示时间上的顺承。However表转折，As a result表结果，For example举例，Now表现在，此处强调动作先后，选D。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
I used to be a night owl and always woke up late. (17)______, I was often late for school and felt tired all day. Last month, I decided to change my lifestyle.

(18)______, I started going to bed at 10 p.m. and waking up at 6 a.m. At first, it was difficult to fall asleep so early. I listened to soft music and stopped using my phone before bed.

(19)______, instead of rushing to school without breakfast, I have time to enjoy a healthy meal. I also do some exercise in the morning, which makes me feel energetic.

The result is amazing. (20)______, I can focus better in class and have more time for hobbies after school. Changing my morning routine has really improved my life.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查时间对比。前文讲述改变初期的困难（ listened to soft music...），此处开始描述现在的状况（have time...），用Now（现在）与过去的困难形成对比，也承接下文的现在时态。However表转折，As a result表结果，For example举例，Then表然后，均不如Now能体现时间转换。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
I used to be a night owl and always woke up late. (17)______, I was often late for school and felt tired all day. Last month, I decided to change my lifestyle.

(18)______, I started going to bed at 10 p.m. and waking up at 6 a.m. At first, it was difficult to fall asleep so early. I listened to soft music and stopped using my phone before bed.

(19)______, instead of rushing to school without breakfast, I have time to enjoy a healthy meal. I also do some exercise in the morning, which makes me feel energetic.

The result is amazing. (20)______, I can focus better in class and have more time for hobbies after school. Changing my morning routine has really improved my life.

questions.explanation: 【解析】考查例证关系。前文说"结果很惊人"，后文"上课能更集中注意力，放学后有时间培养爱好"是列举具体的好结果，用For example（例如）。However表转折，As a result表结果（但前文已提The result），Then表然后，Now表现在，此处是举例说明amazing的具体表现，选C。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
I used to be a night owl and always woke up late. (17)______, I was often late for school and felt tired all day. Last month, I decided to change my lifestyle.

(18)______, I started going to bed at 10 p.m. and waking up at 6 a.m. At first, it was difficult to fall asleep so early. I listened to soft music and stopped using my phone before bed.

(19)______, instead of rushing to school without breakfast, I have time to enjoy a healthy meal. I also do some exercise in the morning, which makes me feel energetic.

The result is amazing. (20)______, I can focus better in class and have more time for hobbies after school. Changing my morning routine has really improved my life.
